skill.name: google-calendar-automation
skill.description: Automate Google Calendar events, scheduling, availability checks, and attendee management via Rube MCP (Composio). Create events, find free slots, manage attendees, and list calendars programmatically.
skill.summary: Automate Google Calendar workflows through Composio's Google Calendar toolkit
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 125 potential issues, all of which are false positives from markdown code formatting. The skill is documentation-only (SKILL.md) with no executable code. The 'external_commands' detections are backticks used for markdown code spans like `GOOGLECALENDAR_CREATE_EVENT`. The single network reference is a legitimate MCP endpoint configuration URL. No security risks identified.

content.user_title: Automate Google Calendar with Claude Code
content.value_statement: Manage your Google Calendar programmatically through Claude Code. Create events, check availability, manage attendees, and sync calendars without leaving your development workflow.
actual_capabilities: Create and update Google Calendar events with ISO 8601 timestamps
Find available time slots using free/busy queries
Search and list events by date range or keywords
Add and remove attendees from existing events
List and manage multiple Google Calendars
limitations: Requires active Google Calendar connection via Rube MCP
Does not support natural language dates (requires ISO 8601 format)
Cannot resolve attendee names to emails (requires email addresses)
Google Meet link creation may fail on personal Gmail accounts

use_cases.title: Schedule Development Sprints
use_cases.description: Create recurring sprint planning events, automatically add team members, and find time slots that work for everyone.

use_cases.title: Calendar-Driven Task Automation
use_cases.description: Check availability before scheduling meetings, avoid conflicts, and maintain a clean calendar workflow.

use_cases.title: Team Meeting Coordination
use_cases.description: Find common free slots across multiple calendars, add attendees, and send meeting invitations.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Event
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Google Calendar event tomorrow at 2pm PST called 'Team Standup' that lasts 30 minutes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic event creation with natural language description

prompt_templates.title: Find Available Time Slots
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all available time slots between 9am and 5pm tomorrow that are at least 1 hour long.

prompt_templates.title: Search and Update Events
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for events titled 'Sprint Review' and add john@example.com as an attendee.
prompt_templates.scenario: Finding events by keyword and modifying attendees

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Calendar Availability
prompt_templates.prompt: Check availability for next week across my primary calendar and work calendar, then find a 2-hour free slot.

output_examples.input: Create a meeting tomorrow at 3pm called 'Code Review'
output_examples.output: Event created successfully: 'Code Review' on 2026-02-26 at 15:00:00 in America/Los_Angeles timezone. Event ID: abc123xyz

output_examples.input: Find free slots this Friday for 1 hour
output_examples.output: Found 3 available slots: 9:00-10:00 AM, 2:00-3:00 PM, and 4:30-5:30 PM on 2026-02-27

output_examples.input: Search for all 'Sprint' events this month

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to verify MCP connection and get current tool schemas
Use IANA timezone identifiers like 'America/New_York' instead of abbreviations like 'EST'
Specify dates in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS) rather than natural language
Search for events by title first to get the event_id before updating or deleting
anti_patterns: Do not use natural language dates like 'tomorrow' or 'next week' in API calls
Do not set event_duration_minutes to 60 or higher; use event_duration_hour instead
Do not assume event titles can be used as identifiers; always search to get event_id
Do not use UTC timestamps (ending in 'Z') for local date queries without timezone offset

faq.question: Does this skill work without the Rube MCP server?
faq.answer: No, this skill requires the Rube MCP server to be connected. Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server in your configuration.

faq.question: Can I use natural language dates like 'tomorrow at 3pm'?
faq.answer: No, the Google Calendar API requires ISO 8601 format dates. You must convert 'tomorrow at 3pm' to '2026-02-26T15:00:00' with the appropriate timezone.

faq.question: How do I add attendees by name instead of email?
faq.answer: The Google Calendar API only accepts email addresses. Use a contact lookup tool like GMAIL_SEARCH_PEOPLE to resolve names to emails first.

faq.question: Why does my event show at the wrong time?
faq.answer: You may be using UTC timestamps (ending in 'Z') instead of local timezone offsets. Use RFC3339 format with offsets like '2026-02-26T15:00:00-08:00' for PST.

faq.question: What is the difference between PATCH_EVENT and UPDATE_EVENT?
faq.answer: PATCH_EVENT updates only specific fields while keeping existing values. UPDATE_EVENT replaces the entire event. Use PATCH for partial updates to avoid losing data.

faq.question: Can this skill create Google Meet links automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, Google Meet link creation is enabled by default. However, it may fail on personal Gmail accounts and will gracefully fall back to creating the event without a meet link.
